title: mentha gentilis var. pratensis
intro: Mentha gentilis var. pratensis, also known as wild mint or field mint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to Europe. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of wild mint include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to alleviate symptoms of allergies and colds. Wild mint can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, essential oils, and tinctures, making it a versatile ingredient in natural remedies.
